note_data.title: Tangent to a Circle
keyPoints: A tangent to a circle is a straight line which touches the circle at one and only one point
The point where tangent touches the circle is called the point of contact or point of tangency
A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius drawn to the point of contact
Two tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length
The angle between a tangent and a chord drawn from the point of contact equals the angle in the alternate segment
From any external point, only two tangents can be drawn to a circle
The tangent at any point on a circle is perpendicular to the diameter passing through that point
Common tangents to two circles can be internal or external depending on their position

definitions.definition: A tangent to a circle is a straight line which touches the circle at one and only one point.
The point where a tangent touches the circle is called the point of contact or point of tangency.
A point which lies outside the circle is called an external point.
A straight line which is tangent to two or more circles is called a common tangent.
The two segments of a circle formed by a chord are called alternate segments.

importantQuestions.question: Prove that tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: A circle with center O and tangent AB touching at point P. To prove: OP ⊥ AB. Proof: If OP is not perpendicular to AB, then perpendicular from O to AB will meet AB at some other point Q. This means OQ < OP, which contradicts that P is the point of contact. Hence, OP ⊥ AB.

importantQuestions.question: Prove that two tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: Circle with center O, external point P, tangents PA and PB touching at A and B. To prove: PA = PB. Construction: Join OA, OB, OP. Proof: In triangles OAP and OBP: OA = OB (radii), ∠OAP = ∠OBP = 90° (tangent ⊥ radius), OP = OP (common). Therefore, △OAP ≅ △OBP (RHS). Hence, PA = PB (corresponding sides of congruent triangles).

mcqs.question: If two tangents are drawn from an external point to a circle, then the angle between the tangents is:

options.C: Supplementary to the angle at center
options.D: Equal to the angle at center

mcqs.question: The length of tangent from external point P to a circle with center O and radius 5 cm is 12 cm. The distance OP is:
A tangent to a circle makes an angle with the radius at point of contact equal to:

commonMistakes: Students often forget that tangent is perpendicular to radius at point of contact
Confusing point of contact with center of circle in diagrams
Not mentioning RHS congruency rule when proving tangent length equality
Drawing tangents from internal point instead of external point
Forgetting to write 'given', 'to prove', and 'construction' in geometry proofs
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 long proof question in BSEK papers
Always draw neat diagrams with proper labeling - marks are given for diagrams
Memorize the standard proofs word-by-word as they appear in STBB textbook
Practice numerical problems involving Pythagorean theorem with tangent lengths
Remember to write complete proof format: Given, To Prove, Construction, Proof
formulas: If tangent length = l, radius = r, distance from external point to center = d, then l² + r² = d²
Length of tangent from external point P to circle = √(OP² - r²)
note_data.keyDiagram: Circle with center O, external point P, two tangents PA and PB touching the circle at points A and B respectively. Join OA, OB, OP to form triangles OAP and OBP. Mark right angles at A and B to show tangent perpendicular to radius.
